response.query_interpretation: Searching for scenes matching: Elimination ceremony drama

results.scene_title: Corbin is eliminated after the Egypt-Corbin confrontation
results.description: At elimination, Corbin argues Egypt shut him out and made communication impossible, while Egypt counters that Corbin shrank and whispered instead of leading.  Ramsay ultimately decides Corbin was too disconnected and not ready to be a head chef, eliminates him, and warns the blue team that their numbers are dwindling.

results.scene_title: Amber becomes the first eliminated chef
results.description: Ramsay eliminates Amber, telling her she does not have what it takes to be his next head chef.  After she hands over her jacket and exits, he warns the remaining cast that the night was a disaster and that more than one person could leave if standards do not rise immediately.

results.scene_title: The blue team turns service failure into a blame war
results.description: Back in the dorms, the blue team debates whether the bigger problem was Brandon’s meat station, Corbin’s passivity, or Egypt’s abrasive control.  By elimination, Kyle announces the nominees as Egypt and Corbin, explicitly tying Egypt to ego and Corbin to a fish station that never came together.

results.scene_title: Ramsay ejects the Blue Team from service
results.description: After the second raw lamb, Ramsay marches the Blue Team into the pantry, questions their passion, and throws them out of service.  The punishment is not just anger; it is a formal declaration that the team has failed the night completely.

results.scene_title: Egypt breaks down over missing his family

results.scene_title: Hannah seals the Red Team win
results.description: With the score still in reach for both teams, the final pairing comes down to Shant and Hannah.  Shant posts a respectable 4, but Hannah's maple leaf duck breast with curry chocolate demi-glace earns a 5 from Ramsay, flipping the ending into a Red Team victory and unleashing the women's celebration.

results.scene_title: Uri declares himself unfit after Shant’s medical exit
results.description: Ramsay first informs both teams that Shant has gone to the hospital and will not continue, then Uri steps forward and explains that his back injury is so severe he can barely stand and continuing would only worsen it.  When Ramsay asks if he is declaring himself unfit to continue, Uri answers yes, and the room absorbs that the blue team has now lost a second chef before dinner service even starts.

resolved_clips.title: Hannah seals the Red Team win
resolved_clips.sceneTitle: Hannah seals the Red Team win
resolved_clips.description: With the score still in reach for both teams, the final pairing comes down to Shant and Hannah.  Shant posts a respectable 4, but Hannah's maple leaf duck breast with curry chocolate demi-glace earns a 5 from Ramsay, flipping the ending into a Red Team victory and unleashing the women's celebration.

resolved_clips.title: Uri declares himself unfit after Shant’s medical exit
resolved_clips.sceneTitle: Uri declares himself unfit after Shant’s medical exit
resolved_clips.description: Ramsay first informs both teams that Shant has gone to the hospital and will not continue, then Uri steps forward and explains that his back injury is so severe he can barely stand and continuing would only worsen it.  When Ramsay asks if he is declaring himself unfit to continue, Uri answers yes, and the room absorbs that the blue team has now lost a second chef before dinner service even starts.
